contextBlurb: British Columbia has been at the forefront of Canadian housing reform. The province has enacted legislation on short-term rental regulation, transit-oriented density, and the elimination of single-family-only zoning. The Residential Tenancy Act provides strong tenant protections, and the Speculation and Vacancy Tax targets foreign and vacant property owners.
